site_title: Ponpon Chen Fan Hub | Official Archive & Latest News
site_description: The unofficial music archive for Ponpon Chen. Explore the musical journey, news coverages, live performances, and radio interviews of this rising jazz singer.

hero.title: Ponpon Chen
hero.subtitle: Jazz Artist
hero.slogan: Telling the stories of every night with a soulful voice.
hero.disclaimer: An unofficial, fan-made archive dedicated to Ponpon's musical journey. We carefully curate her media coverage, radio interviews, and live performances, creating a cozy space for fans to linger and enjoy. For corrections or suggestions, please email: samlin0117@gmail.com

about.title: About Ponpon
about.description:
Ponpon hails from Taipei, Taiwan, and moved to the US at age 20 to study jazz. In the musical world of Ponpon Chen (陳芃瑄), there are no boundaries between instruments and the human voice. With her soulful, evocative vocals as a foundation, combined with fluid guitar accompaniment, she weaves freely through melodies.

Beyond her captivating "musical storytelling" charm, what fascinates fans most is her live performance, where she simultaneously masterfully executes crystal-clear whistling, dynamic scatting, and intricate guitar picking. This "trinity" of synchronized performance—where every note serves as a vessel for storytelling—has become her most recognizable musical signature. With her debut jazz album set for release in 2026, look forward to this delicate yet powerful musical energy.

endorsement.title: Notable Mentions
endorsement.subtitle: MUSICAL CODA
endorsement.krugman_desc: Nobel laureate Paul Krugman has recently featured Ponpon's jazz performance videos in the "Musical Coda" section at the end of his Substack columns, introducing her music to a wide audience of readers who have praised her talent.
endorsement.page_desc: Notable figures who have featured Ponpon's music on their platforms, helping her reach new audiences.

changelog.title: Site Update Log
changelog.subtitle: DEVELOPMENT LOG
changelog.description: Chronicle of major feature updates and content revisions.

items.title: New 'Jazz Repertoire' Launched
items.content: Added the Jazz Repertoire page with search and filter features. Gradually adding in-depth appreciation of classic jazz standards performed by Ponpon, along with links to Ponpon's live performance of the respective jazz song.

items.title: Notable Mentions Page Launched
items.content: Added a dedicated page for celebrity endorsements, featuring repeated mentions by Nobel laureate Paul Krugman.

items.title: Fan Knowledge Quiz Launched
items.content: Introduced an interactive quiz game for fans to test their knowledge about Ponpon's musical journey.

items.title: Video Gallery Optimization
items.content: Implemented year-based grouping to help fans navigate through performances and works more efficiently.

items.title: Trilingual Support Live
items.content: Full site content localized in Traditional Chinese, English, and Japanese.

items.title: Site Development Started
items.content: Official start of the Ponpon Chen Music Hub project.

news.article1: 2025-10-11 Taiwanese Musician Ponpon Chen Featured on ABC Platform (Original: Chinese)
news.article1Desc: 23-year-old Taiwanese musician Ponpon Chen brings her unique jazz guitar and vocal style to ABC News...
news.article2: 2016-08-24 Eelin Star Entertainment Group Champion Ponpon Chen (Original: Chinese)
news.article2Desc: The Entertainment Group championship was won by 14-year-old Ponpon Chen, the youngest winner of this year, who has aspired to be a singer-dancer since childhood...
news.article3: 2025-07-18 Taiwanese Singer Ponpon Chen Featured on US News Outro, Guitar Performance Hits Million Views (Original: Chinese)
news.article3Desc: A 90-second jazz performance by 23-year-old Taiwanese singer Ponpon Chen was featured at the end of an ABC News broadcast, generating 1.2 million views online...

news.article5Desc: Fronting her band with an electric guitar, Taiwanese jazz musician Ponpon Chen then broke into her unique rendition of American musical comedian Barry Mitchell's famous jingle...

news.article6Desc: We had the good fortune of connecting with Ponpon Chen and we've shared our conversation below. Over the past decade, my musical journey has been filled with diverse experiences...

news.article7Desc: A 90-second jazz performance by 23-year-old Taiwanese singer Ponpon Chen was featured at the end of an ABC News broadcast, generating 1.2 million views online...
news.article8: 2025-07-20 Ponpon Chen Transforms from Guitar Novice to Jazz Rising Star, Unexpectedly Meets Masters (Original: Chinese)
news.article8Desc: In just 4 years, 23-year-old Taiwanese girl Ponpon Chen transformed from a guitar novice to a rising jazz star in the US. With a fearless spirit, she worked hard abroad...
news.article9: 2018-05-07 Wang Yuan Personally Selects Taiwanese Girl! "One-on-One Duet Eye Contact" Makes Him Shyly Laugh Twice (Original: Chinese)
news.article9Desc: TFBOYS member Wang Yuan was recently invited to appear on the show "Come Sing with Me 3". The day before recording, he personally called fans to invite them to the show, but was ruthlessly hung up on. Fortunately, he successfully invited 3 fans. The show also featured 6 groups for collaboration, and the final lucky girl chosen for a solo duet was Ponpon Chen from Taiwan.
news.article10: 2025-08-02 Heaven Raven Interview: Taiwanese Girl Ponpon Chen Living in California (Original: Chinese)
news.article10Desc: Taiwanese girl Ponpon Chen, currently living in California, was invited by ABC this May to rearrange the traditional news outro "World News Polka" into jazz. It has accumulated over 1.2 million views online and sparked discussions on Threads. Today, HR is happy to connect with Ponpon in the US to share how it all started.
news.article11: 2015-05-24 Students compete for 'City Star' to win an agency contract and fulfill their dream of becoming a singer (Original: Chinese)
news.article11Desc: Taipei City University of Science and Technology held the '2015 City Star' selection competition. 600 teenagers participated, and Ponpon won 2nd place in this fierce competition.

quiz.title: Ponpon Knowledge Quiz
quiz.description:
How much do you know about Ponpon's jazz journey?
10 random questions, 10 points each. Can you get a perfect 100?
quiz.rank_senior_desc: Incredible! You know everything about Ponpon!
quiz.rank_loyal_desc: Great! You really follow Ponpon's musical journey!
quiz.rank_entry_desc: Not bad! Keep following Ponpon's latest news!
quiz.rank_passing_desc: Welcome to Ponpon's musical world, stay a while!
quiz.rank_visitor_desc: That's okay! It's never too late to start knowing Ponpon!
quiz.rank_newbie_desc: Welcome to the fan world, let's explore together!

questions.question: Which of the following skills is a 'special instrument' Ponpon first performed publicly in LA in April 2025?
questions.explanation: In April 2025, she incorporated the Washboard into her performance at Cicada Restaurant and Lounge for the first time.

questions.question: In which video did Ponpon first publicly demonstrate her 'Scatting' technique?
questions.explanation: She first demonstrated her signature Scatting technique in the 2024 Q3 performance of 'How High The Moon'.

questions.question: What is Ponpon Chen's date of birth?
questions.explanation: Ponpon Chen was born on April 5, 2002.

questions.question: In 2015, in which competition did she win 2nd place while in 7th grade?
questions.explanation: In 2015, she won 2nd place in the 'City Star' competition during her 7th grade year.

questions.question: In 2016, at age 14, which category championship did she win at the 5th Eelin Star finals?
questions.explanation: In 2016, at age 14, Ponpon won the Entertainment Group championship at the 5th Eelin Star finals.

questions.question: What special record did Ponpon set when she won the Eelin Star championship?
questions.explanation: Ponpon became the youngest champion of that year's competition at age 14.

questions.question: When participating in 'Come Sing with Me 3' in 2018, which singer was she chosen to perform a solo duet with?
questions.explanation: In 'Come Sing with Me 3', Ponpon was chosen to perform a solo duet with TFBOYS member Wang Yuan.

questions.question: Which song did Ponpon perform with Wang Yuan in 'Come Sing with Me 3'?
questions.explanation: Ponpon performed the song 'Pride' (驕傲) with Wang Yuan.

questions.question: In 2019, which segment did 17-year-old Ponpon participate in during 'Jungle Voice 2'?
questions.explanation: Ponpon participated as a 'Little Demon' challenger in the Challenger Round of Jungle Voice 2.

questions.question: Which song by Cheer Chen did Ponpon perform in 'Jungle Voice 2'?
questions.explanation: She performed Cheer Chen's 'Somewhere in Taipei', receiving high praise from all three mentors.

questions.question: Which department did Ponpon attend during her time at Fuxing Senior High School?
questions.explanation: In 2017, Ponpon was admitted to the Drama Class at Fuxing Senior High School, known for nurturing artistic talent.

questions.question: During the pandemic, which music legend's video inspired Ponpon to switch from Pop to Jazz and start practicing electric guitar intensely?
questions.explanation: While at NTUA, she was inspired by a George Benson video and decided to take a leave to focus on jazz guitar.

questions.question: After taking a leave from university, which teacher did she study guitar with at 'Riverside Music'?
questions.explanation: Ponpon studied with teacher Geddy Lin (林正如) at Riverside Music after taking a leave from university.

questions.question: Where did Ponpon hold her first solo headline show before moving to the US in 2022?
questions.explanation: In 2022, she held her first solo headline show at Riverside Live House before moving to the US.

questions.question: In Sep 2022, she released her first original MV. This song was actually written when she was 15. What is the title?
options: Finding Someone Like You on the Edge
questions.explanation: Her first original MV, 'Finding Someone Like You on the Edge' (找到一樣在邊緣徘徊中的你), was written when she was 15.

questions.question: Which school did Ponpon attend when she moved to Los Angeles in 2022?
questions.explanation: Ponpon moved to LA to attend the Musicians Institute (MI).

questions.question: What was her major at the Musicians Institute (MI)?
questions.explanation: She majored in Jazz Guitar at MI.

questions.question: In 2023, what occupational injury forced Ponpon to rest for six months due to intense daily practice?
questions.explanation: In 2023, she suffered from Tendonitis due to intense practice and was forced to rest for half a year.

questions.question: At Studio City Sound in LA, which Grammy-winning engineer encouraged her to upload her performance videos?
questions.explanation: She met Grammy-winning engineer Tom Weir at Studio City Sound, who encouraged her to share her videos.

questions.question: Where did Ponpon hold her solo ticketed show during her brief return to Taiwan in Dec 2024?
questions.explanation: In Dec 2024, she held a solo ticketed show at Riverside Live House in Ximen Red House.

questions.question: In May 2025, which ABC late-night program did Ponpon perform on?
questions.explanation: Ponpon was invited to perform on the ABC News late-night show 'World News Now'.

questions.question: Which outro song did she rearrange and perform on the ABC program?
questions.explanation: She rearranged and performed 'World News Polka', and the video reached over a million views.

questions.question: What advice did George Benson personally give Ponpon at his 2025 workshop?
questions.explanation: George Benson personally told her: 'Don't imitate, find your own voice'.

questions.question: Which annual LA event did Ponpon perform at on Dec 24, 2025?
questions.explanation: On Christmas Eve 2025, she was invited to perform at the L.A. County Holiday Celebration.

questions.question: Which Grammy winner did Ponpon perform alongside at the L.A. County Holiday Celebration?
questions.explanation: She performed alongside Grammy winner Randy Waldman.

questions.question: Which junior high school was Ponpon attending during the 2015 'City Star' competition?
questions.explanation: In 2015, she was a student at Renai Junior High School.

questions.question: What important advice did Tom Weir give Ponpon in 2024?
options: Upload multiple performance videos to YouTube
questions.explanation: Tom Weir suggested she upload her videos to YouTube to let more people see her talent.

questions.question: During her injury recovery in the US, what did her teacher suggest she study to maintain her progress?
options: Practice piano with her left hand
Study jazz history and stories behind songs
questions.explanation: During her recovery, her teacher suggested studying jazz history and song backstories.

questions.question: What was Ponpon's initial reaction to receiving the email from ABC News in 2025?
options: Thought it was a scam and couldn't believe it
Immediately called her parents in Taiwan
Felt unprepared and asked for a delay
Rejected it initially due to low pay
questions.explanation: When she first received the email from ABC News, she thought it was a scam.

questions.question: In her ABC News performance, what unique element did she add besides singing, guitar, and scatting?
questions.explanation: In the ABC News performance, Ponpon combined guitar, scatting, and her signature whistling technique.

questions.question: How did Ponpon earn credits to complete her studies at MI while she was unable to play guitar due to injury?
options: Use singing to replace guitar for evaluations
Apply for leave and return to Taiwan
questions.explanation: Unable to play guitar, she used singing as a substitute to earn credits for her practical evaluations.

questions.question: What role does Ponpon believe 'mistakes' play in a jazz performance?
options: Part of improvisation and start of creativity
Must stop and apologize to the audience
questions.explanation: Ponpon believes that in jazz, mistakes are part of improvisation and can even spark new creative ideas.

questions.question: What is Ponpon's favorite color?
questions.explanation: Her frequent use of bright red dresses in live performances shows her love for the color red.

questions.question: Why did the homeroom teacher write a letter to apologize to Ponpon at her junior high school graduation ceremony?
options: Failed to write a recommendation letter for her high school application
Forgot to award her the certificate of merit
Previously opposed her participation in the Campus Million Star singing contest
Didn't believe she could achieve her goal in the entertainment industry
questions.explanation: At the graduation ceremony, her homeroom teacher wrote a letter apologizing for initially not believing she could achieve her goal in the entertainment industry.

questions.question: Before agreeing to let her study in the US, what specific requirement did Ponpon's father make?
options: Get a university degree in Taiwan first
Achieve an IELTS score of 7.0 or higher
Hold a solo ticketed concert in Taiwan first
Create a financial assessment report of annual expenses
questions.explanation: From an investment perspective, her father required her to clearly list tuition and living expenses to evaluate the return on investment.

questions.question: When Ponpon recorded the ABC News outro in the US, where did the idea for the "Intro" signature music come from?
options: A mashup of traditional Taiwanese folk songs and jazz
A classic polka melody she self-taught on YouTube
Imitating the opening style of 1920s jazz
The signature melody hummed by studio owner Tom Weir
questions.explanation: Studio owner Tom Weir hummed the show's signature melody during the recording, which became her inspiration for the Intro.

questions.question: What was the name of the first podcast show that interviewed Ponpon?
questions.explanation: In August 2025, Ponpon was invited for her first podcast interview on the show "Breaking Out of the Do-Zone".

questions.question: Which radio program was Ponpon's first radio interview?
questions.explanation: In April 2021, Ponpon was interviewed by host CafeCat on Radio Taiwan International's program "Music Essence", marking her first-ever solo radio interview.

cheek-to-cheek.title: Cheek to Cheek
cheek-to-cheek.background:
Written specifically for the classic 1935 Hollywood musical film 'Top Hat'. In the 1930s, the United States was overshadowed by the Great Depression, with high unemployment and a bleak social atmosphere. The public desperately needed a sanctuary for their minds, and glamorous Hollywood musicals became the best form of escapism. Accompanied by the elegant, luxurious, and lighthearted duet dance of the film's lead characters, 'Cheek to Cheek' provided audiences with a utopian fantasy far removed from their harsh reality.

The song's atmosphere is incredibly light, dreamy, and intoxicating. The melody and rhythm carry an effortless sense of floating, creating an ultimately romantic ambiance where the entire world seems to stand still, leaving only two lovers under the spotlight, immersed in their spinning dance steps.

'Cheek to Cheek' is more than just a love song; it embodies the idea that: 'Even if reality is imperfect, simply dancing with the one you love feels like being in heaven.'

fly-me-to-the-moon.title: Fly Me to the Moon
fly-me-to-the-moon.background:
This song was originally titled 'In Other Words', but because the recurring chorus line 'Fly me to the moon' became so deeply ingrained in people's minds, it was officially renamed to 'Fly Me to the Moon' in 1963.

Although the song was written in the 1950s, its massive popularity in the 1960s coincided with the peak of the Space Race during the Cold War between the United States and the Soviet Union. At that time, humanity was filled with boundless longing and imagination for the unknown universe. Frank Sinatra's upbeat 1964 version perfectly captured the American public's enthusiasm and optimism about the moon landing.

'Fly Me to the Moon' is a masterpiece that flawlessly combines a 'grand cosmic perspective' with 'microscopic personal emotions.' It tells us that the magic of love is enough to make one feel like flying into space, yet all these miracles stem from a simple holding of hands, a kiss, and a simple 'I love you.'

all-of-me.title: All of Me
all-of-me.background:
📍 Historical Background: An Emotional Outlet During the Great Depression
Born in the 1930s at the onset of the Great Depression in the US, an era of economic hardship, rising unemployment, and immense social pressure, music became a crucial emotional comfort and outlet for the general public. 'All of Me' spread through radio broadcasts and live performances, soothing countless weary souls.

🍸 Cultural Depth: The Jazz Spirit of Turning Sorrow into Romance
Although 'All of Me' is a song about heartbreak, it rejects hysterical, tragic breakdowns. Instead, it carries a unique urban humor and an inescapable sense of heartbreak, perfectly fitting the jazz bar culture of the time—where people were accustomed to drinking while following unpredictable rhythms, transforming life's and love's sorrows into a tipsy romance.

💔 Lyrical Imagery: Ultimate Deprivation and Manifested Pain
The song is most praised for its ingenious lyrics, translating the mental agony of having one's 'heart hollowed out' after a breakup into a sense of physical incompleteness.
The lyrics straightforwardly plead: Since you took the part that used to be my heart, why not take my lips and arms as well? It perfectly depicts the unreserved dedication in love and the profound sense of deprivation upon losing the other person.

on-the-sunny-side.title: On the Sunny Side of the Street
on-the-sunny-side.background:
📍 Historical Background: A Ray of Light in a Turbulent Era
The United States had just experienced the 1929 Wall Street Crash and was stepping into the despairing "Great Depression." As unemployment and poverty shrouded society, this song made its debut in a 1930 Broadway musical. Instead of wallowing in sorrow, it acted like a ray of sunlight breaking through the clouds, injecting a much-needed shot of optimism into the gloomy American society of the time.

☀️ Lyrical Imagery: Change Your Mindset, Leave Worries at the Door
The song's opening is highly visual and action-oriented:
"Grab your coat, and get your hat / Leave your worry on the doorstep"
This represents a proactive kind of optimism. The lyrics encourage people not to linger in dark corners. As long as you are willing to take a step toward the sunny side of the street, life can take a turn for the better. This philosophy of "changing your mindset" fills the song with inspiring power.

👣 Core Meaning: Ultimate Spiritual Wealth
In an era when many struggled to afford their next meal, one of the song's most classic verses gave "wealth" a whole new definition:
"If I never have a cent / I'll be rich as Rockefeller / Gold dust at my feet / On the sunny side of the street."
This verse cleverly compares the sunlight scattered on the ground to gold dust, conveying a profound message: even if your pockets are empty, as long as your heart remains optimistic and sunny, you can be as spiritually wealthy as an oil tycoon.

🎧 Enduring Swing Charm
For jazz singers, this tune is the perfect choice for interacting with the audience and spreading joy and warmth. When the brisk rhythm starts, it truly feels as though it can make people forget their worries and stroll along the sunlit street alongside the singing voice.

timelineItems.title: Musical Beginnings
timelineItems.desc: At age 10, she began street dance training, laying her rhythmic foundation, and discovered her whistling talent. Although this gift was kept hidden for years due to cultural expectations, her musical dreams had already begun to sprout. At 12, driven by her passion for singing, she started entering vocal competitions.

timelineItems.title: Rising Star
timelineItems.desc: Won 2nd place at the "City Star" singing contest in 2015. In 2016, at just 14 years old, she won the Entertainment Group Championship of the 5th "Eelin Star". ➜ Watch highlights

timelineItems.title: Admitted to Drama Class at Fuxing Senior High School
timelineItems.desc: In this renowned institution for nurturing artistic talent, she received systematic training in acting, physical expression, and theater. This solid artistic foundation not only honed her stage presence but also laid a profound groundwork for her uniquely stylized musical performances in the future. ➜ Watch rehearsal highlights

timelineItems.title: Overseas Duet & Landmark Contest
timelineItems.desc:
Performed on the popular TV show "Come Sing with Me 3" in 2018, singing a solo duet with Wang Yuan. ➜ Watch highlights

In 2019, she competed as a challenger in Taiwan's iconic music survival show "Jungle Voice". ➜ Watch highlights

timelineItems.title: Jazz Awakening
timelineItems.desc: Admitted to the Drama Dept at NTUA. Inspired by jazz legend George Benson during pandemic lockdown, she took a leave of absence to passionately self-study jazz electric guitar, deciding to pursue music in the US.

timelineItems.title: Studying in LA & Transformation
timelineItems.desc: Moved to Los Angeles to study Jazz Guitar at the Musicians Institute (MI). Forced to take a six-month break from playing due to tendonitis from intense practice, she used this time to deeply study jazz vocals and music history, beautifully evolving into a versatile, all-around musician.

timelineItems.title: LA Gigs & Social Media Breakthrough
timelineItems.desc: Became active in LA jazz clubs as a Band Leader, accumulating deep live performance experience. Invited by Grammy-winning studio owner Tom Weir to release performance videos on his channel, her unique charm quickly sparked a social media craze with rapidly soaring viewership.

timelineItems.title: First Public SCATTING Performance
timelineItems.desc: During the performance of "How High The Moon", Ponpon debuted her signature Scatting (guitar and vocal unison) technique in a public setting for the first time. ➜ Watch performance

timelineItems.title: First Ticketed Show in the US
timelineItems.desc: Successfully held her first official solo ticketed performance in the US at Agua Caliente Casino in Palm Springs, marking a significant milestone in her musical career.➜ Watch performance

timelineItems.title: First Public Washboard Performance
timelineItems.desc: Incorporated the washboard into her live performance for the very first time at Cicada Restaurant and Lounge in Los Angeles.➜ Watch performance

timelineItems.title: Global Recognition & Master Collaborations
timelineItems.desc:
In May, she was invited to perform a rearranged outro for ABC News "World News Now", with the video surpassing a million views. ➜ Watch performance

At the end of the year, she was invited to an annual L.A. grand event to perform alongside Grammy winner Randy Waldman. ➜ Watch performance

timelineItems.title: Debut Jazz Album & Gibson Spotlight Artist
timelineItems.desc: Synthesizing a decade of musical depth and her US experiences, she is soon to release her debut English jazz studio album, opening a new chapter in her career. That same year, her exceptional guitar skills were recognized by the world's top guitar brand, inviting her to be Gibson's 2026 Spotlight Artist, showcasing the international impact of a new-generation jazz diva.
